loadPolicy.usage: Apply the matching rule groups for files being touched. Do not paste this file into chat unless asked.
loadPolicy.priority: These constraints extend AGENTS.md and docs/realty-ops-engineering-grail.md. If rules conflict, choose the stricter production/security rule.

rules: Use Realty Ops as the internal repo name until a public name is chosen.
Do not use Kova.
Build as a multi-tenant brokerage platform, not a one-off custom automation.
Keep Instagram DMs and comments in the day-one product scope.
Route files in apps/web/src/app must stay thin and compose feature or package services only.
Cross-boundary DTOs and validation schemas belong in packages/core/src/domains.
Provider payload mapping, webhook signature helpers, and integration normalization belong in packages/integrations.
API transport and response validation belong in packages/api-client.
Feature-specific state and product composition belongs in apps/web/src/features.
Reusable app UI belongs in apps/web/src/components.
Do not create prototype, scratch, v2, backup, or duplicate implementation folders inside product paths.
Use strict TypeScript and avoid any.
Use unknown for untrusted input and narrow with Zod or explicit type guards.
Validate untrusted inputs and API responses with Zod at package boundaries.
Derive TypeScript types from schemas where possible instead of maintaining parallel shapes.
Model nullable, optional, and environment-specific values explicitly.
Do not let UI components parse raw provider payloads.
Every public API route must validate request method, body, query, and params before side effects.
Every webhook handler must parse provider payloads into internal events before business logic.
Normalize phone numbers, emails, Instagram usernames, provider IDs, timestamps, and lead source values.
Reject malformed payloads with structured errors and no side effects.
Never trust AI-extracted fields without schema validation.
Trim freeform message text before persistence.
Preserve raw provider payloads only in controlled audit storage when needed.
Redact tokens, phone numbers, emails, and provider secrets from logs.
Do not render raw provider text as HTML.
Normalize outbound SMS content through opt-out and quiet-hour checks before production.

security.baseline: OWASP ASVS for web app and API controls.
rules: Never commit real secrets, service-role keys, access tokens, webhook secrets, or CRM credentials.
Never expose Supabase service-role keys or provider credentials to client code.
Encrypt integration credentials before persistence.
Validate Meta, Twilio, Retell, and internal webhook signatures before production side effects.
Do not log raw tokens, private lead payloads, call recording URLs, or full transcripts in production logs.
Admin overrides and assignment changes must be auditable.
Every tenant-owned table must have RLS enabled before production access.
Keep local, staging, and production data and config separate.
Staging must not write to production CRMs, production Meta apps, or production Twilio numbers.
Add or update .env*.example files when introducing required variables.
Environment readers must fail loudly for missing required runtime config in staging and production.
Production webhook URLs must be stable deployed URLs, not tunnels.
Schema changes must be represented by SQL migrations under supabase/migrations.
Do not make dashboard-only schema changes without migrations.
Every workspace-owned row must include workspace_id.
RLS policies must cover broker admin, assigned agent, unassigned workspace member, and outsider access.
Insert and update policies must use with check for rows being written.
Generate or update typed contracts after DB shape changes when app code consumes those shapes.
Meta events normalize into internal lead events before lead mutation.
Follow Up Boss sync must be queued, retryable, and logged.
Twilio inbound requests require signature validation before production.
Retell call webhooks must validate signature or configured secret before production.
Provider errors should be captured with provider, workspace, event type, and retry metadata.
Provider credentials are workspace scoped and least-privilege where the provider allows it.
Build quiet, premium, operational UI instead of a marketing dashboard.
Use a gray/white neutral-first visual system with sparse status colors.
Use real shadcn/Radix primitives when the web app shell is introduced.
Build custom product components by composing shadcn primitives instead of importing generic dashboard kits.
Use cards only for repeated items, modals, and framed tools.
Prioritize scanability, lead status clarity, assignment clarity, and fast action.
Do not put explanatory product copy inside workflow screens unless it directly helps the task.
Icon-only controls require tooltips and should use lucide-react when available.
Add tests for schemas, normalization, webhook parsing, scoring, assignment, and CRM sync decisions.
Add policy or migration verification before treating sensitive Supabase data as production-ready.
Keep visual components thin enough that model logic can be tested outside React rendering.
Run releaseGate after structural changes and before handoff.
Do not weaken lint, typecheck, or tests to make a feature pass.
CI must run typecheck, lint, tests, and constraints check before staging or production.
Use separate Sentry/PostHog environment labels for development, staging, and production.
Capture errors with enough context to debug but without secrets or sensitive lead payloads.
Document rollback steps for migrations and provider config changes before launch.
Prefer promoting the same tested artifact from staging to production when possible.
Do not add runtime dependencies when platform APIs or existing packages solve the problem cleanly.
Track npm audit output and prioritize high or critical issues.
Keep provider SDKs isolated behind packages/integrations or server-only app code.
Do not import server-only dependencies into client components.
